macro_summary: Wednesday's session saw modest gains (SPY +0.4%) as markets digested the FOMC minutes revealing a majority of officials anticipate rate hikes if Hormuz-driven inflation persists. NVDA reported record Q1 profit of $58.3B after the close, beating on revenue ($81.62B vs $79.18B consensus) and guiding Q2 to ~$91B, but shares fell ~1.3% in extended trading — the bar was priced for perfection. US10Y eased to 4.57% after Tuesday's spike, while the EU finally agreed to implement its US trade deal ahead of Trump's July deadline.
commodities_note: WTI at $99.53 (+0.41%) remains elevated on the Hormuz blockade now in its 12th week; Wood Mackenzie warns a prolonged closure could push Brent toward $200/bbl by year-end. Gold flat at $4,532. DXY steady at 99.15. Commercial oil inventories down to a few weeks' cover per the IEA — the supply stress is real but not yet in a panic phase.

writeup.headline: NVDA beat and guided higher but shares fell — priced-for-perfection bar wasn't cleared.
writeup.what_to_do: Reconciling with pre-earnings setup: entered as priced_for_perfection (vs_sma50 +14.3%, RSI 62). NVDA beat Q1 revenue ($81.62B vs $79.18B consensus, per Yahoo Finance Video) and guided Q2 to ~$91B (vs ~$87B estimates, per Reuters Videos), but the after-hours decline confirms the high bar wasn't cleared. All 3 priors gapped down averaging -1.8%; bear case remains ~$219.45. Pre-earnings band (3-prior avg): no symmetric upside prior exists — all priors moved down. No entry above the $195.49 SMA50. Wait for the post-earnings settle — if the stock holds above $215 after the open, the structural demand thesis (Vera Rubin sampling, $91B guide) is intact but entry still requires a pullback to the SMA50. Calibration note: CAUTION preceded >+5% rallies in 6/10 cases — the name runs hot. Fragility: China H200 license uncertainty and FY-offset calendar effects remain unresolved.

writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if WRD fails to reclaim SMA50 ($7.46) on above-average volume within the next 10 sessions.

singapore.summary: All three banks are extended and overbought with RSI above 70 on declining volume. The US10Y at 4.57% remains firmly above the 4.20% threshold where NIM relief begins — this is day 62 of the compression headwind with no MAS relief until October. OCBC is the relative outperformer on NIM resilience, but none of the three offer an actionable entry at current levels. The cluster is a waiting game for either a rate break or a pullback to the 50-day averages.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired by dual-headwind NIM compression — awaiting US10Y break below 4.20% or MAS policy shift in October.
singapore.key_development: FOMC minutes revealed rate-hike bias if Hormuz inflation persists — this extends the NIM compression timeline for Singapore banks, as US rates staying elevated keeps USD funding costs high.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster rode NVDA's earnings beat into the close (SOXX +0.76% to 520.31) but the after-hours selloff confirms the bar was priced for perfection. The internal dispersion is extreme: AMD (+35% over the month vs cluster) and INTC (+58%) have ripped on hope, while ASML (-16%) and AVGO (-18%) have lagged on legislative and earnings-binary overhangs respectively. SK Hynix surged 11.2% today on NVDA's HBM demand signal. Fifteen of the cluster's names are hard-blocked on technical extension. There are no clean entries in semis right now — the cluster is a momentum vehicle, not a stock-picking opportunity.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically extended — AI demand confirmed by NVDA's $91B guide, but every name is priced above where risk/reward favours entry.
semiconductors.key_development: NVDA guided Q2 to ~$91B (vs ~$87B consensus) and broke out data-center revenue by hyperscaler vs enterprise for the first time — the hyperscaler-ACIE split matters for the cluster because it changes how investors model end-demand concentration.

big_tech.summary: The BigTech cluster is mixed — AMZN caught a bid from NVDA's capex confirmation, MSFT is testing the tight $425.35 resistance, GOOG is extended at +14.6%, and PLTR is the only name below its SMA50 but carries a distorted R:R and a reliably bearish calibration track record. The OpenAI IPO filing news (per OpenTools, May 21) is a double-edged read for MSFT — it validates the AI ecosystem but introduces a public-market competitor that could accelerate AWS/Anthropic diversification away from Azure. No clean entries in BigTech this session.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed by NVDA's guide, but credit-circularity risk (MSFT-OpenAI) and extension across AMZN/GOOG keep entries blocked.
big_tech.key_development: NVDA broke out hyperscaler vs non-hyperscaler data-center revenue for the first time — this directly addresses the Wall Street concern that AMZN/GOOG/MSFT capex concentration is a risk, and the readthrough for BigTech is that NVDA's customer diversification reduces the 'hyperscaler spending peak' narrative.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Received NVDA's matching $2B photonics investment alongside LITE — AI datacenter co-packaged optics read-through. NVDA's $91B guide confirms the bandwidth bottleneck thesis that drives optical interconnect demand.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's post-Nasdaq-100 selloff extends another 5-8% and COHR follows — the photonics thesis is shared, and COHR at S&P 500 scale offers better liquidity.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking upgrades benefit Arista as NVDA's Spectrum-X ecosystem expands. NVDA's breakout of non-hyperscaler data-center revenue directly increases the addressable market for Arista's switching platform.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if ANET pulls back 8-10% from current levels — the NVDA networking readthrough is structural, but Arista's own earnings are the confirmation catalyst.

interconnected.name: Samsung Electronics
interconnected.reason: Third HBM3E supplier after SK Hynix and Micron. Samsung's union suspended a planned strike after reaching a tentative pay deal (per Reuters, May 20), removing a near-term supply disruption risk. HBM qualification status with NVDA is the key read-through on memory supply diversity.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Samsung's HBM3E qualification with NVDA is confirmed — this would break the SK Hynix-Micron duopoly and reprice the memory supply chain.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC designer competing with AVGO and NVDA in the merchant silicon space. NVDA's ACIE revenue segmentation (AI Clouds, Industrial, Enterprise) expands the addressable market for custom silicon — MRVL is the second-largest custom ASIC player behind AVGO.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if AVGO's June 4 earnings confirm custom ASIC demand acceleration above $12B annual run-rate — MRVL would ride the same wave at a smaller scale.

active_risks: Hormuz blockade (Week 12): Iran keeps the Strait closed to non-Iranian shipping since Feb 28 US-Israeli strikes; UAE fast-tracking bypass pipeline at 50% completion (per ynetnews, May 20); IEA warns commercial oil inventories down to 'few weeks' cover; Wood Mackenzie projects Brent could approach $200/bbl by year-end if closure persists.

Fed rate-hike bias: FOMC minutes (May 20) revealed a majority anticipates rate increases if Hormuz-driven inflation stays above 2%; four dissenting votes, the most since 1992; US10Y at 4.57% reflects the market pricing in this hawkish shift.

EU-US trade deal implementation: EU parliament agreed to implement the Turnberry accord ahead of Trump's July 4 deadline (per WSJ, May 20), removing tariff overhang on transatlantic trade; the deal removes import duties on most US goods entering the EU.

China-US limited trade thaw: China agreed to buy 200 Boeing jets and resume US beef imports (per WSJ, May 20), and flagged agricultural tariff cuts (per Successful Farming, May 20), but the broader trade framework remains stalled with no chip export-control relief.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $99.53 keeps an energy premium in equity markets, benefiting defense-exposed names like PLTR while pressuring consumer-discretionary and logistics. The Fed's rate-hike bias is capping tech multiples despite NVDA's record results — the 4.57% US10Y is the ceiling on semis valuations. Trade thaw is modestly positive for TSM and ASML supply chains but insufficient to lift the China-overhang discount. Singapore bank NIM compression extends as US rates stay elevated — day 62 with no relief catalyst in sight.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: FOMC minutes revealed a majority of officials anticipate rate hikes if Hormuz inflation persists — the most hawkish Fed signal since 1992 with four dissenting votes. EU parliament reached a provisional deal to implement the US trade agreement, removing a tariff-overhang risk. China agreed to limited trade concessions (200 Boeing jets, beef imports) but no chip export-control breakthroughs.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Maintain defensive posture — no new entries until the Hormuz situation shows a durable de-escalation path or the US10Y breaks below 4.20%, whichever comes first. The NVDA earnings catalyst has passed; let the post-earnings settle before reassessing entry levels.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Defensive — extension and overbought conditions dominate
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 15 of 22 names are hard-blocked on technical extension, led by the semis cluster where AMD, INTC, MU, and SK Hynix sit >40% above their 50-day averages. The Hormuz-driven inflation keeps the Fed hawkish and the US10Y above 4.20%, blocking NIM relief for Singapore banks. No clean risk/reward setups exist across the watchlist — this is a waiting game for mean reversion across the board.

watch_for_entry.note: R:R 4.1:1 to $8.33 with entry at $6.85–$7.14. SMA50 reclaim above $7.46 with volume triggers entry. ADR delisting risk caps position size — starter only.

on_deck.note: PEG 1.25 with 35% revenue growth at 20.6x forward PE. CoWoS packaging sold out through end-2026 — structural monopoly on AI capacity. Reassess on pullback to $370.63 (SMA50) with volume.
on_deck.rationale: PEG 1.25, forward PE 20.6x below cluster median, revenue growth 35.1% — fundamentally attractive but technically extended at +8.4% above SMA50.
on_deck.trigger: Pullback to $370.63 (50-day SMA) with above-average volume, or a breakout above $414.50 resistance with volume confirmation.

[Rebucketed CAUTION → avoid (fwd PE 46.1x >= 20x (no PEG); FCF yield 0.41% <= 2%)] +32.3% extreme extension at 46.1x forward PE with 15.5% revenue growth — overshot fundamentals. Reassess on pullback to $200 (SMA50).

[Rebucketed CAUTION → avoid (PEG 2.47 >= 1.5; FCF yield 1.87% <= 2%)] +57.3% extreme extension after 11.2% surge on NVDA results. RCP review pending: 11.6% gap detected; thesis flag uncommitted. Reassess on pullback to ₩1.5M.

[Rebucketed CAUTION → avoid (PEG 1.83 >= 1.5; FCF yield 0.34% <= 2%)] NVDA's beat is an indirect readthrough for AWS — catalyst confirmation period incomplete. Reassess on pullback to $239.67 (SMA50).

hold_no_action.note: Lock-up expiry overhang keeps CBRS in no-man's-land. Wait for the float to clear and a stable price to form before reassessing entry.

new_stocks_to_watch.note: Add to watchlist if LITE's selloff extends another 5-8% — shared NVDA photonics investment thesis. COHR at S&P 500 scale offers better liquidity than LITE for the same optical interconnect exposure.
Add to watchlist if ANET pulls back 8-10% — NVDA's non-hyperscaler revenue segmentation directly expands Arista's addressable market in enterprise AI networking.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Custom ASIC revenue accelerates above $12B annual run-rate with OpenAI/ByteDance inference ramp confirmed — guidance raise.
bullish_upgrades: AVGO → WATCH (ASIC thesis confirmed, post-earnings pullback entry)
MRVL → watchlist candidate (custom ASIC second-wave beneficiary)
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Custom ASIC growth decelerates or VMware integration misses — guidance cut on hyperscaler in-housing fears.
bearish_impact: AVGO → deeper CAUTION (thesis impairment — custom ASIC moat questioned)
Semis cluster → remains blocked (negative readthrough to NVDA/AMD custom silicon narrative)

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Fed holds rates steady with dovish language — acknowledges Hormuz inflation as transitory, signals readiness to cut if growth slows.
bullish_upgrades: D05_SI → WATCH candidate (US10Y breaks below 4.20% on dovish Fed — NIM relief trigger)
O39_SI → WATCH candidate (same NIM relief mechanism)
U11_SI → WATCH candidate (same NIM relief mechanism)
NVDA → WATCH candidate (rate relief lifts tech multiples from current extension)
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Fed hikes 25bp or explicitly guides for hikes — confirms the FOMC minutes hawkish bias, pushing US10Y above 4.80%.
bearish_impact: Singapore banks → deeper CAUTION (US10Y surge extends NIM compression timeline beyond October MAS)
QQQ/SPY → risk-off (rate shock compresses growth multiples across BigTech and Semis)
PLTR → remains blocked (defense spending resilient but multiple compression hits high-PE names hardest)

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Durable ceasefire with Strait reopening timeline confirmed — WTI below $85, shipping insurance normalises.
bullish_upgrades: All watchlist → extension blocks re-evaluate (energy-premium removal resets inflation expectations, Fed turns dovish)
Singapore banks → NIM relief accelerates (rate-cut expectations pull US10Y below 4.20%)
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Escalation to direct US-Iran naval combat or Strait closure extends through end-2026 — WTI above $130 sustained.
bearish_impact: All watchlist → deeper CAUTION (supply-shock recession risk eclipses stock-level analysis)
BE → potential WATCH (on-site power generation demand spikes on grid instability, but entry still gated on pullback)

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
